sourceMovie.title: A Few Good Men
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Few Good Men (1992) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Rob Reiner's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Shutter Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Few Good Men clicked for you, Shutter Island (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Few Good Men?
faq.answer: Try Shutter Island, Erin Brockovich, Purple Hearts—each shares DNA with A Few Good Men while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Few Good Men?
faq.answer: A Few Good Men (1992) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Few Good Men worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
